blocks.body: Requests / Offers / Suggestions helps you build polite requests, offers of help, and soft ideas for shared action.

{"level":"A2-B1","intro":"In this topic you will see how to build polite requests, offers, and suggestions with can, could, would, shall, let's, and why don't we.","rules":[{"label":"REQUESTS","color":"emerald","text":"Can, could, and would help you ask for something directly or more softly.","example":"Can you help me? / Could you open the door?"},{"label":"OFFERS","color":"blue","text":"Shall I...? and Would you like...? work well for offers of help or options.","example":"Shall I carry that bag? / Would you like some tea?"},{"label":"SUGGESTIONS","color":"amber","text":"Let's, Why don't we...?, and Shall we...? make a gentle suggestion for shared action.","example":"Let's start now. / Why don't we take a break?"}]}

{"title":"1. Useful patterns","intro":"These are some of the most common frames for everyday polite interaction.","items":[{"label":"Requests","title":"Can / Could you + V?","subtitle":"Can you wait? / Could you help me?"},{"label":"Polite requests / offers","title":"Would you + V? / Would you like...?","subtitle":"Would you send it? / Would you like some coffee?"},{"label":"Offers","title":"Shall I + V?","subtitle":"Shall I open the window?"},{"label":"Suggestions","title":"Let's... / Why don't we...? / Shall we...?","subtitle":"Let's go. / Why don't we try again?"}]}

{"title":"2. How politeness changes","sections":[{"label":"Requests","color":"emerald","description":"Can you...? sounds more direct. Could you...? and Would you...? are often softer and more polite.","examples":[{"en":"Can you send me the link?","ua":"Can you send me the link?"},{"en":"Could you send me the link, please?","ua":"Could you send me the link, please?"},{"en":"Would you check this for me?","ua":"Would you check this for me?"}],"note":"The softer the wording, the more polite it often sounds."},{"label":"Offers of help","color":"blue","description":"For an offer, Shall I...? and Would you like...? are very useful: one offers an action, the other offers an option or thing.","examples":[{"en":"Shall I carry your bag?","ua":"Shall I carry your bag?"},{"en":"Shall I call a taxi?","ua":"Shall I call a taxi?"},{"en":"Would you like some water?","ua":"Would you like some water?"}]},{"label":"Shared suggestions","color":"amber","description":"Let's, Why don't we...?, and Shall we...? help you suggest a shared plan without sounding too pushy.","examples":[{"en":"Let's start with the easy part.","ua":"Let's start with the easy part."},{"en":"Why don't we meet after lunch?","ua":"Why don't we meet after lunch?"},{"en":"Shall we go now?","ua":"Shall we go now?"}],"note":"Let's sounds more direct, while Why don't we...? and Shall we...? are a bit softer."}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using to after a modal request form.","wrong":"Can you to help me?","right":"✅ Can you help me?","hint":"After can / could / would, use the bare infinitive."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Adding an extra subject after let's.","wrong":"Let's we start now.","right":"✅ Let's start now.","hint":"After let's, go straight to the verb."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Mixing up an offer with a request in a shall form.","wrong":"Shall you open the window?","right":"✅ Shall I open the window? / Could you open the window?","hint":"Shall I...? offers help. For a request to another person, use Could you...?."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick rule","items":["Can you...? = direct request. Could you...? / Would you...? = softer request.","Shall I...? = an offer of help from the speaker.","Would you like...? = a polite offer of an option or thing.","Let's..., Why don't we...?, and Shall we...? = a suggestion for shared action.","The form you choose changes not only the grammar, but also the level of politeness."]}
